CVE-2010-4237.title: Mercurial before 1.6.4 fails to verify the Common Name field of S ...
CVE-2010-4237.description: Mercurial before 1.6.4 fails to verify the Common Name field of SSL certificates which allows remote attackers who acquire a certificate signed by a Certificate Authority to perform a man-in-the-middle attack.

CVE-2014-9462.title: The _validaterepo function in sshpeer in Mercurial before 3.2.4 a ...
CVE-2014-9462.description: The _validaterepo function in sshpeer in Mercurial before 3.2.4 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a crafted repository name in a clone command.

CVE-2016-3069.title: Mercurial before 3.7.3 allows remote attackers to execute arbitra ...
CVE-2016-3069.description: Mercurial before 3.7.3 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted name when converting a Git repository.

CVE-2016-3105.title: The convert extension in Mercurial before 3.8 might allow context ...
CVE-2016-3105.description: The convert extension in Mercurial before 3.8 might allow context-dependent attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted git repository name.

CVE-2017-1000115.title: Mercurial prior to version 4.3 is vulnerable to a missing symlink ...
CVE-2017-1000115.description: Mercurial prior to version 4.3 is vulnerable to a missing symlink check that can malicious repositories to modify files outside the repository

CVE-2017-1000116.title: Mercurial prior to 4.3 did not adequately sanitize hostnames pass ...
CVE-2017-1000116.description: Mercurial prior to 4.3 did not adequately sanitize hostnames passed to ssh, leading to possible shell-injection attacks.

CVE-2017-17458.title: In Mercurial before 4.4.1, it is possible that a specially malfor ...
CVE-2017-17458.description: In Mercurial before 4.4.1, it is possible that a specially malformed repository can cause Git subrepositories to run arbitrary code in the form of a .git/hooks/post-update script checked into the repository. Typical use of Mercurial prevents construction of such repositories, but they can be created programmatically.

CVE-2018-1000132.title: Mercurial version 4.5 and earlier contains a Incorrect Access Con ...
CVE-2018-1000132.description: Mercurial version 4.5 and earlier contains a Incorrect Access Control (CWE-285) vulnerability in Protocol server that can result in Unauthorized data access. This attack appear to be exploitable via network connectivity. This vulnerability appears to have been fixed in 4.5.1.

CVE-2018-13346.description: The mpatch_apply function in mpatch.c in Mercurial before 4.6.1 incorrectly proceeds in cases where the fragment start is past the end of the original data, aka OVE-20180430-0004.

CVE-2018-13347.title: mpatch.c in Mercurial before 4.6.1 mishandles integer addition an ...
CVE-2018-13347.description: mpatch.c in Mercurial before 4.6.1 mishandles integer addition and subtraction, aka OVE-20180430-0002.

CVE-2016-3105.description: The convert extension in Mercurial before 3.8 might allow context-dependent attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted git repository name.

CVE-2017-17458.description: In Mercurial before 4.4.1, it is possible that a specially malformed repository can cause Git subrepositories to run arbitrary code in the form of a .git/hooks/post-update script checked into the repository. Typical use of Mercurial prevents construction of such repositories, but they can be created programmatically.

CVE-2019-3902.title: A flaw was found in Mercurial before 4.9. It was possible to use  ...
CVE-2019-3902.description: A flaw was found in Mercurial before 4.9. It was possible to use symlinks and subrepositories to defeat Mercurial's path-checking logic and write files outside a repository.
